unit.name: Parsing cues, valency, and projectivity constraints
unit.description: Identifies what information helps a parser decide dependencies and explains the difference between projective and nonprojective structures.
unit.summary: Before presenting a parser, the lecture asks what evidence should guide dependency decisions. The main cues are lexical plausibility between the words at the ends of an arc, the distance between them, what appears in between them, and the valency patterns of heads such as verbs that normally expect certain numbers and arrangements of arguments [ts=2798s] [ts=2875s]. The segment also notes a structural regularity: many dependencies nest cleanly, but human languages can also exhibit crossing dependencies, as in displaced wh-questions or cases where one modifier belongs inside another span [ts=3132s] [ts=3189s]. These crossing cases are called nonprojective dependencies, but the parser used in the assignment will simplify matters by handling only projective dependency parsing [ts=3162s] [ts=3212s].

key_points.text: Useful parsing evidence includes the lexical compatibility of a candidate head-dependent pair.
Dependency distance and intervening material matter because most dependencies are short and rarely span verbs or punctuation.
Valency captures how many arguments a head tends to take and on which sides.
Nonprojective dependencies involve crossings, but the lecture's parser restricts itself to projective parsing.

kp_catalog.name: Local parser state and features
kp_catalog.description: The representation of a parser decision state using the stack, buffer, nearby words, parts of speech, dependency labels, and other local structural cues.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because local parser state and features clarifies the representation of a parser decision state using the stack, buffer, nearby words, parts of speech, dependency labels, and other local structural cues, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Dependency trees and projectivity
kp_catalog.description: The structural constraints on dependency parses, including single-root trees, nesting dependencies, and the distinction between projective and nonprojective arcs.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because dependency trees and projectivity clarifies the structural constraints on dependency parses, including single-root trees, nesting dependencies, and the distinction between projective and nonprojective arcs, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
